tool_events.output:
1→date: July 19, 2024
     2→
     3→behavior_changes:
     4→- area: thread_local
     5→  change: |
     6→    Changes the behavior of the ``SlotImpl`` class destructor. With this change the destructor can be called on any thread.
     7→    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.allow_slot_destroy_on_worker_threads``
     8→    to ``false``.
     9→- area: ext_proc
    10→  change: |
    11→    Adding support for
    12→    :ref:`route_cache_action <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor.route_cache_action>`.
    13→    It specifies the route action to be taken when an external processor response is received in response to request headers.
    14→- area: http2
    15→  change: |
    16→    Changes the default value of ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_oghttp2`` to ``true``. This changes the codec used for HTTP/2
    17→    requests and responses. This behavior can be reverted by setting the feature to ``false``.
    18→- area: http3
    19→  change: |
    20→    Added a happy eyeballs feature to HTTP/3 upstream, where it assuming happy eyeballs sorting results in alternating address
    21→    families will attempt the first v4 and v6 address before giving up on HTTP/3.  This change can be reverted by setting
    22→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http3_happy_eyeballs`` to ``false``.
    23→- area: http2
    24→  change: |
    25→    Passes HTTP/2 ``DATA`` frames through a different codec API. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime
    26→    feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_visitor_for_data`` to ``false``.
    27→- area: runtime
    28→  change: |
    29→    Rejecting invalid YAML. This has been an ``ENVOY_BUG`` linked to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/27434
    30→    for over a year with no hard-blockers so should be Ok. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting
    31→    the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.reject_invalid_yaml`` to ``false`` but the runtime guard must be
    32→    parsed before any invalid YAML.
    33→- area: proxy_protocol
    34→  change: |
    35→    Populate typed metadata by default in proxy protocol listener. Typed metadata can be consumed as
    36→    :ref:`TlvsMetadata type <envoy_v3_api_msg_data.core.v3.TlvsMetadata>`.
    37→    This change can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime flag
    38→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_typed_metadata_in_proxy_protocol_listener`` to ``false``.
    39→- area: composite_filter
    40→  change: |
    41→    Adding support for
    42→    :ref:`sample_percent <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.composite.v3.ExecuteFilterAction.sample_percent>`.
    43→    It specifies the probability of the action execution. If not specified, it is 100%.
    44→- area: golang
    45→  change: |
    46→    Move ``Continue``, ``SendLocalReply`` and ``RecoverPanic`` from ``FilterCallbackHandler`` to ``DecoderFilterCallbacks`` and
    47→    ``EncoderFilterCallbacks``, to support full-duplex processing.
    48→- area: tracing/datadog
    49→  change: |
    50→    Disabled remote configuration by default.
    51→    To enable this feature, use the :ref:`remote_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.trace.v3.DatadogConfig.remote_config>` field.
    52→- area: local_ratelimit
    53→  change: |
    54→    Updated the token bucket implementation to use non-timer based token bucket. The tokens will be refilled when
    55→    the token bucket is accessed and no dependency on the timer.
    56→    This behavior can be temporarily reverted by setting the runtime guard
    57→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_timer_based_rate_limit_token_bucket`` to ``false``.
    58→
    59→minor_behavior_changes:
    60→- area: ext_proc
    61→  change: |
    62→    Timeout errors in external processor now returns ``504 Gateway Timeout`` to downstream clients.
    63→    The previous behavior was returning ``500 Internal Server Error``.
    64→- area: dfp
    65→  change: |
    66→    Changed dynamic forward proxy so local reply errors include DNS resolution details. This behavior can be temporarily
    67→    disabled by setting the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_details`` to ``false``.
    68→- area: grpc
    69→  change: |
    70→    Changes in ``AsyncStreamImpl`` and ``GoogleAsyncStreamImpl`` now propagate tracing context headers in bidirectional streams when using
    71→    :ref:`Envoy gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.envoy_grpc>` or
    72→    :ref:`Google C++ gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.google_grpc>`. Previously, tracing context headers
    73→    were not being set when calling external services such as ext_proc.
    74→- area: http
    75→  change: |
    76→    Fixed host header changes for shadow requests to properly handle ipv6 addresses.
    77→- area: tracers
    78→  change: |
    79→    Set status code for OpenTelemetry tracers (previously unset).
    80→- area: config
    81→  change: |
    82→    Stricter validation of a ``google.protobuf.Duration`` field in a config, rejecting invalid values (where the number
    83→    of years is over 292). This can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
    84→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.strict_duration_validation`` to ``false``.
    85→- area: xds
    86→  change: |
    87→    Updated xDS-TP path naming to better comply with RFC-3986. Encoded resource paths can now include an a colon ``:``,
    88→    instead of ``%3A``. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag
    89→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.xdstp_path_avoid_colon_encoding`` to ``false``.
    90→- area: udp
    91→  change: |
    92→    Change GRO read buffer to 64kB to avoid ``MSG_TRUNC``. And change the way to limit the number of packets processed per event
    93→    loop to work with GRO. This behavior can be reverted by setting runtime guard
    94→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.udp_socket_apply_aggregated_read_limit`` to ``false``.
    95→- area: statistics
    96→  change: |
    97→    Hot restart statistics like ``hot_restart_epoch`` are only set when hot restart is enabled.
    98→- area: dns
    99→  change: |
   100→    Changes the behavior of the ``getaddrinfo`` DNS resolver so that it treats ``EAI_NODATA`` and ``EAI_NONAME``
   101→    as successful queries with empty results, instead of as DNS failures. This change brings the
   102→    ``getaddrinfo`` behavior in-line with the c-ares resolver behavior. This behavior can be reverted by
   103→    setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_nodata_noname_is_success`` to ``false``.
   104→- area: access_log
   105→  change: |
   106→    The upstream connection address, rather than the upstream host address, will be used for the ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS%``,
   107→    ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_PORT%`` and ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS_WITHOUT_PORT%`` access log format specifiers.
   108→    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   109→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.upstream_remote_address_use_connection`` to ``false``.
   110→- area: access_log
   111→  change: |
   112→    The ``%CEL%`` formatter supports call functions.
   113→- area: http
   114→  change: |
   115→    Changing header validation checks in the substitution format utility and CEL code to do RFC-compliant header validation.
   116→    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.consistent_header_validation`` to ``false``.
   117→- area: quic
   118→  change: |
   119→    Cache source/destination address instances in a LRU cache for packet read to improve performance.
   120→    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   121→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.quic_upstream_socket_use_address_cache_for_read`` to ``false``.
   122→- area: quic
   123→  change: |
   124→    When a quic connection socket is created, the socket's detected transport protocol will be set to ``quic``.
   125→- area: config
   126→  change: |
   127→    In xDS configuration, the :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>` value now means
   128→    :ref:`V3 <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.V3>`. :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>`
   129→    is the default value of the enum, so this field may be omitted from all configurations now.
   130→- area: filters
   131→  change: |
   132→    Set ``WWW-Authenticate`` header for ``401`` responses from the Basic Auth filter.
   133→- area: jwt_authn
   134→  change: |
   135→    jwt_authn now validates provider URIs. If the validation is too strict it can temporarily be
   136→    disabled by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_validate_uri`` to
   137→    ``false``.
   138→- area: http
   139→  change: |
   140→    Removed runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.refresh_rtt_after_request`` and legacy code path.
   141→- area: http
   142→  change: |
   143→    Changing HTTP/2 semi-colon prefixed headers to being sanitized by Envoy code rather than nghttp2. Should be a functional no-op but
   144→    guarded by ``envoy.reloadable_features.sanitize_http2_headers_without_nghttp2``.
   145→- area: http
   146→  change: |
   147→    Envoy will now proxy ``104`` headers from upstream, though as with ``100`` only the first 1xx response
   148→    headers will be sent. ``104`` headers are designated by IETF's draft-ietf-httpbis-resumable-upload RFC.
   149→    This behavioral can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard
   150→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_104`` to ``false``.
   151→- area: jwt_authn
   152→  change: |
   153→    Changes the behavior of the
   154→    :ref:`forward <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.forward>`
   155→    config. Previously, the config only removes JWT if set in headers. With this addition, the config can also be
   156→    used to remove JWT set in query parameters. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard
   157→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_remove_jwt_from_query_params`` to ``false``.
   158→- area: custom response filter
   159→  change: |
   160→    Prevent the redirect policy from sending ``100`` as the overridden response code as the custom response must
   161→    be complete.
   162→- area: http
   163→  change: |
   164→    Reject messages with chunked transfer encoding with chunk extension containing ``CR`` not followed by ``LF``. This can be
   165→    temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_disallow_lone_cr_in_chunk_extension``
   166→    to ``false``.
   167→- area: quic
   168→  change: |
   169→    A change in QUICHE has renamed all flags formerly of the form ``quic_reloadable_flag_...`` to ``quiche_reloadable_flag_...``.
   170→    Likewise, for ``quic_restart_flag_...``, now ``quiche_restart_flag_...``. Consequently, all Envoy flags of those forms with an
   171→    ``envoy_quic_...`` prefix have been similarly renamed to ``envoy_quiche_...``.
   172→- area: hashing
   173→  change: |
   174→    Change murmurHash2 hashing on big endian platforms to generate the same values as little endian platforms.
   175→- area: gcp_authn
   176→  change: |
   177→    Use the fixed URL format instead of one from the configuration. This behavior can be disabled by setting runtime flag
   178→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.gcp_authn_use_fixed_url`` to false.
   179→
   180→bug_fixes:
   181→- area: admission control
   182→  change: |
   183→    Fixed the thread-local controller's average RPS calculation to be calculated over the full
   184→    lookback window. Previously, the controller would calculate the average RPS over the amount of
   185→    time elapsed since the oldest valid request sample. This change brings the behavior in line with
   186→    the documentation.
   187→- area: outlier detection
   188→  change: |
   189→    Fixed :ref:`successful_active_health_check_uneject_host
   190→    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.successful_active_health_check_uneject_host>`.
   191→    Before, a failed health check could uneject the host if the ``FAILED_ACTIVE_HC`` health flag had not been set.
   192→- area: quic
   193→  change: |
   194→    Applied 2 QUICHE patches for crash bugs in ``QuicSpdyStream`` ``OnDataAvailable()`` and ``OnInitialHeaderComplete()``.
   195→- area: quic
   196→  change: |
   197→    Fixed crash bug when QUIC downstream stream was read closed and then timed out.
   198→- area: tls
   199→  change: |
   200→    Fix a ``RELEASE_ASSERT`` when using :ref:`auto_sni <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.UpstreamHttpProtocolOptions.auto_sni>`
   201→    if the downstream request ``:authority`` was longer than 255 characters.
   202→- area: tracing
   203→  change: |
   204→    Fix an issue where span id is missing from OpenTelemetry access log entries.
   205→- area: ext_authz
   206→  change: |
   207→    Added field
   208→    :ref:`validate_mutations <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.validate_mutations>`,
   209→    which, when set to ``true``, adds header and query parameter mutation validation to the HTTP ext_authz
   210→    filter. If an authz response contains invalid mutations, the filter responds to the downstream
   211→    request with HTTP ``500 Internal Server Error``. If you use ext_authz with an untrusted side stream,
   212→    it's recommended you set this to ``true``.
   213→- area: http
   214→  change: |
   215→    Fix a crash when reloading the HTTP Connection Manager via ECDS.
   216→- area: cares
   217→  change: |
   218→    Upgraded c-ares library to 1.20.1 and added fix to c-ares DNS implementation to additionally check for ``ARES_EREFUSED``,
   219→    ``ARES_ESERVFAIL`` and ``ARES_ENOTIMP`` status. Without this fix, ``DestroyChannelOnRefused`` and
   220→    ``CustomResolverValidAfterChannelDestruction`` unit test will break.
   221→- area: udp
   222→  change: |
   223→    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when updates to a pre-existing cluster were made (e.g. ``HostSet`` changes).
   224→- area: ext_authz
   225→  change: |
   226→    Handle ``append_action`` from :ref:`external authorization service <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.auth.v3.CheckResponse>`
   227→    that was ignored.
   228→- area: oauth2
   229→  change: |
   230→    Fixed a bug that would cause Envoy to crash when receiving an Oauth callback while the Oauth upstream is unhealthy
   231→    (e.g. due to DNS issues).
   232→- area: http
   233→  change: |
   234→    Fix BalsaParser resetting state too early, guarded by
   235→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_delay_reset`` which defaults to ``true``.
   236→- area: ext_authz
   237→  change: |
   238→    Set the SNI value from the requested server name if it isn't available on the connection/socket. This applies when
   239→    ``include_tls_session`` is ``true``. The requested server name is set on a connection when filters such as the TLS
   240→    inspector are used.
   241→- area: oauth
   242→  change: |
   243→    The ID token cookie now expires at the same time the id token itself expires, instead of when the access token expires.
   244→- area: decompression
   245→  change: |
   246→    Fixed a bug where Envoy will go into an endless loop when using the brotli decompressor. If the input stream has
   247→    redundant data, the decompressor will loop forever.
   248→- area: websocket
   249→  change: |
   250→    Only ``101`` is considered a successful response for websocket handshake for HTTP/1.1, and Envoy as a proxy will proxy the response
   251→    header from upstream to downstream and then close the request if other status is received. This behavior can be
   252→    reverted by ``envoy_reloadable_features_check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake``.
   253→- area: async http client
   254→  change: |
   255→    Added one option to disable the response body buffering for mirror request. Also introduced a 32MB cap for the response
   256→    buffer, which can be changed by the runtime flag ``http.async_response_buffer_limit`` based on the product needs.
   257→- area: ext_authz
   258→  change: |
   259→    Validate http service
   260→    :ref:`path_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.HttpService.path_prefix>`,
   261→    Valid HTTP service ``path_prefix`` configuration must start with ``/``.
   262→- area: local_ratelimit
   263→  change: |
   264→    Fixed a bug where the local rate limit filter would crash when the
   265→    :ref:`enable_x_ratelimit_headers <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.ratelimit.v3.RateLimit>`
   266→    is set to ``DRAFT_VERSION_03`` and a send local reply is triggered before the rate limit filter is executed.
   267→- area: admin
   268→  change: |
   269→    Fixed missing :ref:`additional addresses <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.AdditionalAddress>`
   270→    for :ref:`LbEndpoint <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LbEndpoint.endpoint>` in config dump.
   271→- area: tracing
   272→  change: |
   273→    Added support to configure a static config resource detector for the OpenTelemetry tracer.
   274→- area: http
   275→  change: |
   276→    Fixed a bug where additional :ref:`cookie attributes <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.route.v3.RouteAction.HashPolicy.cookie>`
   277→    are not sent properly to clients.
   278→- area: datadog
   279→  change: |
   280→    Bumped the version of datadog to resolve a crashing bug in earlier versions of the library.
   281→- area: lua
   282→  change: |
   283→    Fixed a bug where the user data will reference a dangling pointer to the Lua state and cause a crash.
   284→

   286→- area: tls
   287→  change: |
   288→    Removed ``envoy.reloadable_features.enable_intermediate_ca`` runtime flag and lagacy code paths.

   290→  change: |

   317→  change: |

   361→
   362→new_features:
   363→- area: redis
   364→  change: |
   365→    Added support for all Bloom 1.0.0 commands.
   366→- area: redis
   367→  change: |
   368→    Added support for ``xack``, ``xadd``, ``xautoclaim``, ``xclaim``, ``xdel``, ``xlen``, ``xpending``,
   369→    ``xrange``, ``xrevrange``, ``xtrim``.
   370→- area: hot_restart
   371→  change: |
   372→    Added new command-line flag :option:`--skip-hot-restart-parent-stats`.
   373→- area: matching
   374→  change: |
   375→    Added :ref:`Filter State Input <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.matching.common_inputs.network.v3.FilterStateInput>`
   376→    for matching HTTP input based on filter state objects.
   377→- area: ext_authz
   378→  change: |
   379→    Added :ref:`disallowed_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.disallowed_headers>`
   380→    to specify headers that should never be sent to the external authentication service. Overrides
   381→    :ref:`allowed_headers <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.allowed_headers>`
   382→    if a header matches both.
   383→- area: upstream
   384→  change: |
   385→    Added a new field to ``LocalityLbEndpoints``, :ref:`LocalityLbEndpoints.Metadata
   386→    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LocalityLbEndpoints.metadata>`, that may be used for transport socket
   387→    matching groups of endpoints.
   388→- area: quic
   389→  change: |
   390→    Added support for QUIC server preferred address when there is a DNAT between the client and Envoy. See
   391→    :ref:`new config
   392→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.quic.server_preferred_address.v3.FixedServerPreferredAddressConfig.AddressFamilyConfig.dnat_address>`.
   393→- area: cares
   394→  change: |
   395→    Added :ref:`udp_max_queries<envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.network.dns_resolver.cares.v3.CaresDnsResolverConfig.udp_max_queries>`
   396→    option to limit the number of UDP queries.
   397→- area: http
   398→  change: |
   399→    Added :ref:`disable_shadow_host_suffix_append
   400→    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.RequestMirrorPolicy.disable_shadow_host_suffix_append>`
   401→    in :ref:`request_mirror_policies <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.request_mirror_policies>`
   402→    for disabling appending of the ``-shadow`` suffix to the shadowed host/authority header.
   403→- area: http
   404→  change: |
   405→    Added field :ref:`match_upstream <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.SchemeHeaderTransformation.match_upstream>`,
   406→    which, when set to ``true``, will set the downstream request ``:scheme`` to match the upstream transport protocol.
   407→- area: redis
   408→  change: |
   409→    Added support for `inline commands <https://redis.io/docs/reference/protocol-spec/#inline-commands>`_.
   410→- area: proxy_protocol
   411→  change: |
   412→    Added field :ref:`stat_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.listener.proxy_protocol.v3.ProxyProtocol.stat_prefix>`
   413→    to the proxy protocol listener filter configuration, allowing for differentiating statistics when multiple proxy
   414→    protocol listener filters are configured.
   415→- area: aws_lambda
   416→  change: |
   417→    The ``aws_lambda`` filter now supports the
   418→    :ref:`credentials <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.aws_lambda.v3.Config.credentials>` parameter.
   419→    This enables setting AWS credentials from the filter configuration.
   420→- area: access_log
   421→  change: |
   422→    Added support for :ref:`%UPSTREAM_HOST_NAME% <config_access_log_format_upstream_host_name>` for the upstream host
   423→    identifier.
   424→- area: access_loggers
   425→  change: |
   426→    Added ``TRACE_ID`` :ref:`access log formatter <config_access_log_format>`.
   427→- area: healthcheck
   428→  change: |
   429→    Added support to healthcheck with ProxyProtocol in TCP Healthcheck by setting
   430→    :ref:`health_check_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.TcpHealthCheck.proxy_protocol_config>`.
   431→- area: local_rate_limit
   432→  change: |
   433→    Added support for :ref:`local cluster rate limit
   434→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.local_ratelimit.v3.LocalRateLimit.local_cluster_rate_limit>`.
   435→    If set, the token buckets of the local rate limit will be shared across all the Envoy instances in the local
   436→    cluster.
   437→- area: ext_authz
   438→  change: |
   439→    Added
   440→    :ref:`decoder_header_mutation_rules <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.decoder_header_mutation_rules>`
   441→    which allows you to configure what decoder header mutations are allowed from the ext_authz
   442→    service as well as whether to fail the downstream request if disallowed mutations are requested.
   443→- area: access_log
   444→  change: |
   445→    added new ``access_log`` command operators to retrieve upstream connection information change: ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_URI_SAN%``,
   446→    ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_IP_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_DNS_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_URI_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_DNS_SAN%``,
   447→    ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_IP_SAN%``.
   448→- area: wasm
   449→  change: |
   450→    Update WASM filter to support use as an upstream filter.
   451→- area: open_telemetry
   452→  change: |
   453→    Added :ref:`stat_prefix
   454→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.stat_prefix>`
   455→    configuration to support additional stat prefix for the OpenTelemetry logger.
   456→- area: thrift
   457→  change: |
   458→    Added implementation of :ref:`thrift to metadata <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.thrift_to_metadata.v3.ThriftToMetadata>`
   459→    HTTP filter.
   460→- area: open_telemetry
   461→  change: |
   462→    Added :ref:`formatters
   463→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.formatters>`
   464→    configuration to support extension formatter for the OpenTelemetry logger.
   465→- area: routing
   466→  change: |
   467→    Added support in :ref:`file datasource <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.DirectResponseAction.body>` implementation
   468→    to listen to file changes and dynamically update the response when :ref:`watched_directory
   469→    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.datasource.watched_directory>`
   470→    is configured in :ref:`DataSource <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.datasource>`.
   471→- area: outlier detection
   472→  change: |
   473→    Added :ref:`always_eject_one_host<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.always_eject_one_host>`
   474→    to optionally override the :ref:`max_ejection_percent<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_percent>`.
   475→- area: listener
   476→  change: |
   477→    Added :ref:`bypass_overload_manager <envoy_v3_api_field_config.listener.v3.Listener.bypass_overload_manager>`
   478→    to bypass the overload manager for a listener. When set to ``true``, the listener will not be subject to overload protection.
   479→- area: ext_authz
   480→  change: |
   481→    Added
   482→    :ref:`enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion
   483→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion>`,
   484→    which allows ext_authz to be configured to ignore dynamic metadata in ext_authz responses.
   485→- area: rbac
   486→  change: |
   487→    The RBAC filter will now log the enforced rule to the dynamic metadata field
   488→    ``enforced_effective_policy_id`` and the result to the dynamic metadata field
   489→    ``enforced_engine_result``. These are only populated if a non-shadow engine exists.
   490→- area: jwt_authn
   491→  change: |
   492→    Added :ref:`strip_failure_response
   493→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtAuthentication.strip_failure_response>`
   494→    to allow stripping the failure response details from the JWT authentication filter.
   495→- area: formatter
   496→  change: |
   497→    Added formatters for :ref:`METADATA(VIRTUAL_HOST)<envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.formatter.metadata.v3.Metadata>`.
   498→- area: quic
   499→  change: |
   500→    Added new interface to ``QuicListenerFilter`` that is called on receiving the first packet.
   501→- area: quic
   502→  change: |
   503→    Added :ref:`DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig
   504→    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.quic.server_preferred_address.v3.DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig>` for cases when
   505→    the control plane does not know the correct configuration for the server preferred address.
   506→- area: tls
   507→  change: |
   508→    Added support to match against ``OtherName`` SAN-type under :ref:`match_typed_subject_alt_names
   509→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.CertificateValidationContext.match_typed_subject_alt_names>`.
   510→    An additional field ``oid`` is added to :ref:`SubjectAltNameMatcher
   511→    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.SubjectAltNameMatcher>` to support this change.
   512→- area: retry
   513→  change: |
   514→    Added :ref:`reset-before-request retry policy <config_http_filters_router_x-envoy-retry-on>`.
   515→- area: ext_proc
   516→  change: |
   517→    Added support for observability mode which deprecates ``async_mode``. If enabled, each part of the HTTP request or response
   518→    specified by ``ProcessingMode`` is sent without waiting for the response from the ext_proc service. It is "Send and Go" mode
   519→    that can be used by external processor to observe Envoy data and status.
   520→- area: grpc
   521→  change: |
   522→    Added support for flow control in Envoy gRPC side stream. This behavior can be disabled by setting the runtime flag
   523→    ``envoy.reloadable_features.grpc_side_stream_flow_control`` to ``false``.
   524→- area: oauth
   525→  change: |
   526→    Added :ref:`disable_id_token_set_cookie <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.oauth2.v3.OAuth2Config.disable_id_token_set_cookie>`
   527→    to disable setting the ID Token cookie.
   528→- area: dns_filter
   529→  change: |
   530→    Added support for wildcard resolution in :ref:`inline_dns_table
   531→    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.udp.dns_filter.v3.DnsFilterConfig.ServerContextConfig.inline_dns_table>`
   532→    when DNS filter is working in server mode.
   533→- area: quic
   534→  change: |
   535→    QUIC stream reset error code will be added to transport failure reason.
   536→    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.report_stream_reset_error_code``
   537→    to ``false``.
   538→
   539→deprecated:
   540→- area: tracing
   541→  change: |
   542→    Disable OpenCensus by default, as it is
   543→    `no longer supported/maintained upstream <https://opentelemetry.io/blog/2023/sunsetting-opencensus/>`_.
   544→    This extension can be replaced with the OpenTelemetry tracer and collector.
   545→

With this change the destructor can be called on any thread.\n    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.allow_slot_destroy_on_worker_threads``\n    to ``false``.\n- area: ext_proc\n  change: |\n    Adding support for\n    :ref:`route_cache_action <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_proc.v3.ExternalProcessor.route_cache_action>`.\n    It specifies the route action to be taken when an external processor response is received in response to request headers.\n- area: http2\n  change: |\n    Changes the default value of ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_oghttp2`` to ``true``. This changes the codec used for HTTP/2\n    requests and responses. This behavior can be reverted by setting the feature to ``false``.\n- area: http3\n  change: |\n    Added a happy eyeballs feature to HTTP/3 upstream, where it assuming happy eyeballs sorting results in alternating address\n    families will attempt the first v4 and v6 address before giving up on HTTP/3.  This change can be reverted by setting\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.http3_happy_eyeballs`` to ``false``.\n- area: http2\n  change: |\n    Passes HTTP/2 ``DATA`` frames through a different codec API. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime\n    feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.http2_use_visitor_for_data`` to ``false``.\n- area: runtime\n  change: |\n    Rejecting invalid YAML. This has been an ``ENVOY_BUG`` linked to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/27434\n    for over a year with no hard-blockers so should be Ok. This behavior can be temporarily disabled by setting\n    the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.reject_invalid_yaml`` to ``false`` but the runtime guard must be\n    parsed before any invalid YAML.\n- area: proxy_protocol\n  change: |\n    Populate typed metadata by default in proxy protocol listener. Typed metadata can be consumed as\n    :ref:`TlvsMetadata type <envoy_v3_api_msg_data.core.v3.TlvsMetadata>`.\n    This change can be temporarily disabled by setting the runtime flag\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.use_typed_metadata_in_proxy_protocol_listener`` to ``false``.\n- area: composite_filter\n  change: |\n    Adding support for\n    :ref:`sample_percent <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.composite.v3.ExecuteFilterAction.sample_percent>`.\n    It specifies the probability of the action execution. If not specified, it is 100%.\n- area: golang\n  change: |\n    Move ``Continue``, ``SendLocalReply`` and ``RecoverPanic`` from ``FilterCallbackHandler`` to ``DecoderFilterCallbacks`` and\n    ``EncoderFilterCallbacks``, to support full-duplex processing.\n- area: tracing/datadog\n  change: |\n    Disabled remote configuration by default.\n    To enable this feature, use the :ref:`remote_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.trace.v3.DatadogConfig.remote_config>` field.\n- area: local_ratelimit\n  change: |\n    Updated the token bucket implementation to use non-timer based token bucket. The tokens will be refilled when\n    the token bucket is accessed and no dependency on the timer.\n    This behavior can be temporarily reverted by setting the runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.no_timer_based_rate_limit_token_bucket`` to ``false``.\n\nminor_behavior_changes:\n- area: ext_proc\n  change: |\n    Timeout errors in external processor now returns ``504 Gateway Timeout`` to downstream clients.\n    The previous behavior was returning ``500 Internal Server Error``.\n- area: dfp\n  change: |\n    Changed dynamic forward proxy so local reply errors include DNS resolution details. This behavior can be temporarily\n    disabled by setting the runtime feature ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_details`` to ``false``.\n- area: grpc\n  change: |\n    Changes in ``AsyncStreamImpl`` and ``GoogleAsyncStreamImpl`` now propagate tracing context headers in bidirectional streams when using\n    :ref:`Envoy gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.envoy_grpc>` or\n    :ref:`Google C++ gRPC client <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.GrpcService.google_grpc>`. Previously, tracing context headers\n    were not being set when calling external services such as ext_proc.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Fixed host header changes for shadow requests to properly handle ipv6 addresses.\n- area: tracers\n  change: |\n    Set status code for OpenTelemetry tracers (previously unset).\n- area: config\n  change: |\n    Stricter validation of a ``google.protobuf.Duration`` field in a config, rejecting invalid values (where the number\n    of years is over 292). This can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.strict_duration_validation`` to ``false``.\n- area: xds\n  change: |\n    Updated xDS-TP path naming to better comply with RFC-3986. Encoded resource paths can now include an a colon ``:``,\n    instead of ``%3A``. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.xdstp_path_avoid_colon_encoding`` to ``false``.\n- area: udp\n  change: |\n    Change GRO read buffer to 64kB to avoid ``MSG_TRUNC``. And change the way to limit the number of packets processed per event\n    loop to work with GRO. This behavior can be reverted by setting runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.udp_socket_apply_aggregated_read_limit`` to ``false``.\n- area: statistics\n  change: |\n    Hot restart statistics like ``hot_restart_epoch`` are only set when hot restart is enabled.\n- area: dns\n  change: |\n    Changes the behavior of the ``getaddrinfo`` DNS resolver so that it treats ``EAI_NODATA`` and ``EAI_NONAME``\n    as successful queries with empty results, instead of as DNS failures. This change brings the\n    ``getaddrinfo`` behavior in-line with the c-ares resolver behavior. This behavior can be reverted by\n    setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.dns_nodata_noname_is_success`` to ``false``.\n- area: access_log\n  change: |\n    The upstream connection address, rather than the upstream host address, will be used for the ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS%``,\n    ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_PORT%`` and ``%UPSTREAM_REMOTE_ADDRESS_WITHOUT_PORT%`` access log format specifiers.\n    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.upstream_remote_address_use_connection`` to ``false``.\n- area: access_log\n  change: |\n    The ``%CEL%`` formatter supports call functions.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Changing header validation checks in the substitution format utility and CEL code to do RFC-compliant header validation.\n    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.consistent_header_validation`` to ``false``.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    Cache source/destination address instances in a LRU cache for packet read to improve performance.\n    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.quic_upstream_socket_use_address_cache_for_read`` to ``false``.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    When a quic connection socket is created, the socket's detected transport protocol will be set to ``quic``.\n- area: config\n  change: |\n    In xDS configuration, the :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>` value now means\n    :ref:`V3 <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.V3>`. :ref:`AUTO <envoy_v3_api_enum_value_config.core.v3.ApiVersion.AUTO>`\n    is the default value of the enum, so this field may be omitted from all configurations now.\n- area: filters\n  change: |\n    Set ``WWW-Authenticate`` header for ``401`` responses from the Basic Auth filter.\n- area: jwt_authn\n  change: |\n    jwt_authn now validates provider URIs. If the validation is too strict it can temporarily be\n    disabled by setting the runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_validate_uri`` to\n    ``false``.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Removed runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.refresh_rtt_after_request`` and legacy code path.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Changing HTTP/2 semi-colon prefixed headers to being sanitized by Envoy code rather than nghttp2. Should be a functional no-op but\n    guarded by ``envoy.reloadable_features.sanitize_http2_headers_without_nghttp2``.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Envoy will now proxy ``104`` headers from upstream, though as with ``100`` only the first 1xx response\n    headers will be sent. ``104`` headers are designated by IETF's draft-ietf-httpbis-resumable-upload RFC.\n    This behavioral can be temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.proxy_104`` to ``false``.\n- area: jwt_authn\n  change: |\n    Changes the behavior of the\n    :ref:`forward <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtProvider.forward>`\n    config. Previously, the config only removes JWT if set in headers. With this addition, the config can also be\n    used to remove JWT set in query parameters. This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime guard\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.jwt_authn_remove_jwt_from_query_params`` to ``false``.\n- area: custom response filter\n  change: |\n    Prevent the redirect policy from sending ``100`` as the overridden response code as the custom response must\n    be complete.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Reject messages with chunked transfer encoding with chunk extension containing ``CR`` not followed by ``LF``. This can be\n    temporarily reverted by setting runtime guard ``envoy.reloadable_features.http1_balsa_disallow_lone_cr_in_chunk_extension``\n    to ``false``.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    A change in QUICHE has renamed all flags formerly of the form ``quic_reloadable_flag_...`` to ``quiche_reloadable_flag_...``.\n    Likewise, for ``quic_restart_flag_...``, now ``quiche_restart_flag_...``. Consequently, all Envoy flags of those forms with an\n    ``envoy_quic_...`` prefix have been similarly renamed to ``envoy_quiche_...``.\n- area: hashing\n  change: |\n    Change murmurHash2 hashing on big endian platforms to generate the same values as little endian platforms.\n- area: gcp_authn\n  change: |\n    Use the fixed URL format instead of one from the configuration. This behavior can be disabled by setting runtime flag\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.gcp_authn_use_fixed_url`` to false.\n\nbug_fixes:\n- area: admission control\n  change: |\n    Fixed the thread-local controller's average RPS calculation to be calculated over the full\n    lookback window. Previously, the controller would calculate the average RPS over the amount of\n    time elapsed since the oldest valid request sample. This change brings the behavior in line with\n    the documentation.\n- area: outlier detection\n  change: |\n    Fixed :ref:`successful_active_health_check_uneject_host\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.successful_active_health_check_uneject_host>`.\n    Before, a failed health check could uneject the host if the ``FAILED_ACTIVE_HC`` health flag had not been set.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    Applied 2 QUICHE patches for crash bugs in ``QuicSpdyStream`` ``OnDataAvailable()`` and ``OnInitialHeaderComplete()``.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    Fixed crash bug when QUIC downstream stream was read closed and then timed out.\n- area: tls\n  change: |\n    Fix a ``RELEASE_ASSERT`` when using :ref:`auto_sni <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.UpstreamHttpProtocolOptions.auto_sni>`\n    if the downstream request ``:authority`` was longer than 255 characters.\n- area: tracing\n  change: |\n    Fix an issue where span id is missing from OpenTelemetry access log entries.\n- area: ext_authz\n  change: |\n    Added field\n    :ref:`validate_mutations <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.validate_mutations>`,\n    which, when set to ``true``, adds header and query parameter mutation validation to the HTTP ext_authz\n    filter. :ref:`request_mirror_policies <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.request_mirror_policies>`\n    for disabling appending of the ``-shadow`` suffix to the shadowed host/authority header.\n- area: http\n  change: |\n    Added field :ref:`match_upstream <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.SchemeHeaderTransformation.match_upstream>`,\n    which, when set to ``true``, will set the downstream request ``:scheme`` to match the upstream transport protocol.\n- area: redis\n  change: |\n    Added support for `inline commands <https://redis.io/docs/reference/protocol-spec/#inline-commands>`_.\n- area: proxy_protocol\n  change: |\n    Added field :ref:`stat_prefix <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.listener.proxy_protocol.v3.ProxyProtocol.stat_prefix>`\n    to the proxy protocol listener filter configuration, allowing for differentiating statistics when multiple proxy\n    protocol listener filters are configured.\n- area: aws_lambda\n  change: |\n    The ``aws_lambda`` filter now supports the\n    :ref:`credentials <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.aws_lambda.v3.Config.credentials>` parameter.\n    This enables setting AWS credentials from the filter configuration.\n- area: access_log\n  change: |\n    Added support for :ref:`%UPSTREAM_HOST_NAME% <config_access_log_format_upstream_host_name>` for the upstream host\n    identifier.\n- area: access_loggers\n  change: |\n    Added ``TRACE_ID`` :ref:`access log formatter <config_access_log_format>`.\n- area: healthcheck\n  change: |\n    Added support to healthcheck with ProxyProtocol in TCP Healthcheck by setting\n    :ref:`health_check_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.TcpHealthCheck.proxy_protocol_config>`.\n- area: local_rate_limit\n  change: |\n    Added support for :ref:`local cluster rate limit\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.local_ratelimit.v3.LocalRateLimit.local_cluster_rate_limit>`.\n    If set, the token buckets of the local rate limit will be shared across all the Envoy instances in the local\n    cluster.\n- area: ext_authz\n  change: |\n    Added\n    :ref:`decoder_header_mutation_rules <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.decoder_header_mutation_rules>`\n    which allows you to configure what decoder header mutations are allowed from the ext_authz\n    service as well as whether to fail the downstream request if disallowed mutations are requested.\n- area: access_log\n  change: |\n    added new ``access_log`` command operators to retrieve upstream connection information change: ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_URI_SAN%``,\n    ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_IP_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_PEER_DNS_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_URI_SAN%``, ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_DNS_SAN%``,\n    ``%UPSTREAM_LOCAL_IP_SAN%``.\n- area: wasm\n  change: |\n    Update WASM filter to support use as an upstream filter.\n- area: open_telemetry\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`stat_prefix\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.stat_prefix>`\n    configuration to support additional stat prefix for the OpenTelemetry logger.\n- area: thrift\n  change: |\n    Added implementation of :ref:`thrift to metadata <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.http.thrift_to_metadata.v3.ThriftToMetadata>`\n    HTTP filter.\n- area: open_telemetry\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`formatters\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.access_loggers.open_telemetry.v3.OpenTelemetryAccessLogConfig.formatters>`\n    configuration to support extension formatter for the OpenTelemetry logger.\n- area: routing\n  change: |\n    Added support in :ref:`file datasource <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.DirectResponseAction.body>` implementation\n    to listen to file changes and dynamically update the response when :ref:`watched_directory\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.datasource.watched_directory>`\n    is configured in :ref:`DataSource <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.datasource>`.\n- area: outlier detection\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`always_eject_one_host<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.always_eject_one_host>`\n    to optionally override the :ref:`max_ejection_percent<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_percent>`.\n- area: listener\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`bypass_overload_manager <envoy_v3_api_field_config.listener.v3.Listener.bypass_overload_manager>`\n    to bypass the overload manager for a listener. When set to ``true``, the listener will not be subject to overload protection.\n- area: ext_authz\n  change: |\n    Added\n    :ref:`enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.ext_authz.v3.ExtAuthz.enable_dynamic_metadata_ingestion>`,\n    which allows ext_authz to be configured to ignore dynamic metadata in ext_authz responses.\n- area: rbac\n  change: |\n    The RBAC filter will now log the enforced rule to the dynamic metadata field\n    ``enforced_effective_policy_id`` and the result to the dynamic metadata field\n    ``enforced_engine_result``. These are only populated if a non-shadow engine exists.\n- area: jwt_authn\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`strip_failure_response\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.jwt_authn.v3.JwtAuthentication.strip_failure_response>`\n    to allow stripping the failure response details from the JWT authentication filter.\n- area: formatter\n  change: |\n    Added formatters for :ref:`METADATA(VIRTUAL_HOST)<envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.formatter.metadata.v3.Metadata>`.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    Added new interface to ``QuicListenerFilter`` that is called on receiving the first packet.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig\n    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.quic.server_preferred_address.v3.DataSourceServerPreferredAddressConfig>` for cases when\n    the control plane does not know the correct configuration for the server preferred address.\n- area: tls\n  change: |\n    Added support to match against ``OtherName`` SAN-type under :ref:`match_typed_subject_alt_names\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.CertificateValidationContext.match_typed_subject_alt_names>`.\n    An additional field ``oid`` is added to :ref:`SubjectAltNameMatcher\n    <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.transport_sockets.tls.v3.SubjectAltNameMatcher>` to support this change.\n- area: retry\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`reset-before-request retry policy <config_http_filters_router_x-envoy-retry-on>`.\n- area: ext_proc\n  change: |\n    Added support for observability mode which deprecates ``async_mode``. If enabled, each part of the HTTP request or response\n    specified by ``ProcessingMode`` is sent without waiting for the response from the ext_proc service. It is \"Send and Go\" mode\n    that can be used by external processor to observe Envoy data and status.\n- area: grpc\n  change: |\n    Added support for flow control in Envoy gRPC side stream. This behavior can be disabled by setting the runtime flag\n    ``envoy.reloadable_features.grpc_side_stream_flow_control`` to ``false``.\n- area: oauth\n  change: |\n    Added :ref:`disable_id_token_set_cookie <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.http.oauth2.v3.OAuth2Config.disable_id_token_set_cookie>`\n    to disable setting the ID Token cookie.\n- area: dns_filter\n  change: |\n    Added support for wildcard resolution in :ref:`inline_dns_table\n    <envoy_v3_api_field_extensions.filters.udp.dns_filter.v3.DnsFilterConfig.ServerContextConfig.inline_dns_table>`\n    when DNS filter is working in server mode.\n- area: quic\n  change: |\n    QUIC stream reset error code will be added to transport failure reason.\n    This behavior can be reverted by setting the runtime flag ``envoy.reloadable_features.report_stream_reset_error_code``\n    to ``false``.\n\ndeprecated:\n- area: tracing\n  change: |\n    Disable OpenCensus by default, as it is\n    `no longer supported/maintained upstream <https://opentelemetry.io/blog/2023/sunsetting-opencensus/>`_.\n    This extension can be replaced with the OpenTelemetry tracer and collector.\n", "numLines": 545, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 545}}
